methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $78M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
WeaveGrid is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Apoorv Bhargava and John Taggart that has raised $78M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six same-stage, same-sector companies including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc., alongside one public comparable (LNZA).

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $78M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a valuation of $148M. Peer-median analysis identifies six comparable private companies at Series B stage in Climate & Energy with a median valuation of $355M, ranging from $120M at the low end to $5.0B at the high end (driven by Commonwealth Fusion Systems as an outlier). The peer median reflects broader market calibration for the sector and stage.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $126M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $252M. The dispersion reflects material uncertainty around WeaveGrid's technology maturity, revenue traction, and competitive positioning relative to the peer cohort. The wide peer range ($120M–$5.0B) indicates heterogeneity in investor expectations within frontier-energy infrastructure, suggesting that stage and sector alone are insufficient to narrow the band without visibility into unit economics and product-market validation metrics.
